topImprovements.title: 1.1.1 Non-text Content
topImprovements.description: Image element is missing an alt attribute. Screen readers cannot convey its meaning.
topImprovements.recommendation: Add a descriptive alt attribute: . For decorative images use alt="".
topImprovements.page_title: Top Civil Engineering College in Coimbatore

topImprovements.title: 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value
topImprovements.description: Button element has no accessible name (no text, no aria-label, no title). Screen readers will announce it as an unlabelled button.
topImprovements.recommendation: Add descriptive text inside the button or use aria-label: <button aria-label="Close dialog">
topImprovements.page_title: Top Civil Engineering College in Coimbatore

topImprovements.title: 2.4.1 Bypass Blocks
topImprovements.description: No skip navigation link detected. Keyboard users must tab through the entire navigation menu on every page.
topImprovements.recommendation: Add a skip link as the first focusable element: Skip to main content
topImprovements.page_title: Top Civil Engineering College in Coimbatore

pages.title: Best Engineering Colleges in Coimbatore,Tamilnadu | KCE

uxIssues.description: Modal or dialog detected without a clearly visible close/dismiss button. Users feel trapped with no escape route.
uxIssues.recommendation: Add a clearly visible close button: <button aria-label="Close dialog" class="close-btn">×</button> and support the Escape key.

uxIssues.description: Page has 2 unlabeled <nav> elements. Users and screen readers cannot distinguish between primary, secondary, and footer navigation.
uxIssues.recommendation: Add aria-label to each nav element: <nav aria-label="Primary navigation">, <nav aria-label="Footer links">

uxIssues.description: Page mixes <button> elements and <a> links styled as buttons for primary CTAs. This creates inconsistent interaction patterns.
uxIssues.recommendation: Use <button> for actions (submitting, triggering behavior) and <a href> for navigation. Avoid styling <a> tags as buttons for non-navigation actions.

uxIssues.description: Icon-only button detected with no text label or tooltip. Users must recall the meaning of the icon from memory.
uxIssues.recommendation: Add a visible label or tooltip: <button aria-label="Add to cart"><svg>...</svg> Add to Cart</button>

uxIssues.heuristic: Heuristic #7: Flexibility and Efficiency of Use
uxIssues.description: Deep page (2+ levels) detected without breadcrumb navigation. Users cannot easily understand their location in the site hierarchy.
uxIssues.recommendation:
Add breadcrumb navigation:
  1. Home
  2. Current Page

uxIssues.description: Primary navigation has 60 links, exceeding the cognitive load limit of 7±2 items.
uxIssues.recommendation: Group navigation items into logical categories. Use dropdown menus or mega-menus to organize secondary pages under fewer top-level items.

a11yIssues.description: Image element is missing an alt attribute. Screen readers cannot convey its meaning.
a11yIssues.recommendation: Add a descriptive alt attribute: . For decorative images use alt="".

a11yIssues.description: No skip navigation link detected. Keyboard users must tab through the entire navigation menu on every page.
a11yIssues.recommendation: Add a skip link as the first focusable element: Skip to main content

a11yIssues.description: Button element has no accessible name (no text, no aria-label, no title). Screen readers will announce it as an unlabelled button.
a11yIssues.recommendation: Add descriptive text inside the button or use aria-label: <button aria-label="Close dialog">

a11yIssues.description: Button element has no accessible name (no text, no aria-label, no title). Screen readers will announce it as an unlabelled button.
a11yIssues.recommendation: Add descriptive text inside the button or use aria-label: <button aria-label="Close dialog">

a11yIssues.description: Link text "read more" is ambiguous. Screen reader users cannot determine its destination out of context.
a11yIssues.recommendation: Replace with descriptive text or add aria-label: Read more

a11yIssues.description: Heading level jumps from H3 to H5, skipping level(s). This breaks the document outline for assistive technologies.
a11yIssues.recommendation: Add intermediate heading level(s) between H3 and H5.

personas.name: First-time Visitor
personas.friction: Some inconsistency in the interface creates minor confusion, but the main flow is discoverable.
personas.recommendation: Add contextual hints for form fields and improve CTA hierarchy. Consider a welcome modal for first-time visitors.

personas.name: Elderly User
personas.friction: Clear typography and simple layout make this page accessible and comfortable for elderly users.
personas.recommendation: Maintain current accessibility practices. Consider adding a font-size toggle for users who need larger text.

personas.name: Power User
personas.friction: Core efficiency features are missing in some areas but the main workflow is functional.
personas.recommendation: Add persistent breadcrumb navigation and improve search functionality. Consider adding keyboard navigation hints.

personas.name: Visually Impaired User
personas.role: Screen reader and assistive technology user
personas.friction: Good accessibility practices make this page usable with screen readers. Minor improvements can be made.
personas.recommendation: Conduct regular screen reader testing. Consider adding 'aria-live' regions for dynamic content updates.

personas.name: Frequent Customer
personas.friction: Some inconsistencies slow down repeat tasks but core functionality works reliably.
personas.recommendation: Ensure navigation consistency across all pages. Add breadcrumbs to help returning users track their location.

before.css:
/* No accessible alt text */
img { display: block; }
before.visual: The image renders visually but is completely invisible to screen readers. Users relying on assistive technology receive no information about this image.

after.visual: The image now has descriptive alt text. Screen readers will announce the image content, making it accessible to visually impaired users.

personas.name: First-time Visitor
personas.friction: Clear structure and visible CTAs make this page very easy for new visitors to understand and engage with.
personas.recommendation: Maintain clarity with a consistent design system. Consider A/B testing the hero CTA copy for higher conversion.

personas.name: Elderly User
personas.friction: Clear typography and simple layout make this page accessible and comfortable for elderly users.
personas.recommendation: Maintain current accessibility practices. Consider adding a font-size toggle for users who need larger text.

personas.name: Power User
personas.friction: Efficient navigation and clear structure allow power users to complete tasks quickly.
personas.recommendation: Enhance power-user features with keyboard shortcuts and advanced filtering options.

personas.name: Visually Impaired User
personas.role: Screen reader and assistive technology user
personas.friction: Good accessibility practices make this page usable with screen readers. Minor improvements can be made.
personas.recommendation: Conduct regular screen reader testing. Consider adding 'aria-live' regions for dynamic content updates.

personas.name: Frequent Customer
personas.friction: Consistent interface and efficient navigation help returning users complete tasks quickly and confidently.
personas.recommendation: Consider adding personalization features for returning users, such as saved preferences and quick-access shortcuts.

personas.name: First-time Visitor
personas.friction: Clear structure and visible CTAs make this page very easy for new visitors to understand and engage with.
personas.recommendation: Maintain clarity with a consistent design system. Consider A/B testing the hero CTA copy for higher conversion.

personas.name: Elderly User
personas.friction: Clear typography and simple layout make this page accessible and comfortable for elderly users.
personas.recommendation: Maintain current accessibility practices. Consider adding a font-size toggle for users who need larger text.

personas.name: Power User
personas.friction: Efficient navigation and clear structure allow power users to complete tasks quickly.
personas.recommendation: Enhance power-user features with keyboard shortcuts and advanced filtering options.

personas.name: Visually Impaired User
personas.role: Screen reader and assistive technology user
personas.friction: Good accessibility practices make this page usable with screen readers. Minor improvements can be made.

personas.name: Frequent Customer
personas.friction: Consistent interface and efficient navigation help returning users complete tasks quickly and confidently.
personas.recommendation: Consider adding personalization features for returning users, such as saved preferences and quick-access shortcuts.

before.css:
/* No accessible alt text */
img { display: block; }
before.visual: The image renders visually but is completely invisible to screen readers. Users relying on assistive technology receive no information about this image.

after.visual: The image now has descriptive alt text. Screen readers will announce the image content, making it accessible to visually impaired users.

before.visual: The image renders visually but is completely invisible to screen readers. Users relying on assistive technology receive no information about this image.

after.visual: The image now has descriptive alt text. Screen readers will announce the image content, making it accessible to visually impaired users.

pages.title: Best college for Engineering In Tamilnadu - KCE

uxIssues.description: Modal or dialog detected without a clearly visible close/dismiss button. Users feel trapped with no escape route.
uxIssues.recommendation: Add a clearly visible close button: <button aria-label="Close dialog" class="close-btn">×</button> and support the Escape key.

uxIssues.description: Page has 3 H1 headings. Standard convention is one H1 per page representing the main topic.
uxIssues.recommendation:
Use exactly one

per page. Demote secondary titles to

or use a visually styled

element.

uxIssues.description: Page has 2 unlabeled <nav> elements. Users and screen readers cannot distinguish between primary, secondary, and footer navigation.
uxIssues.recommendation: Add aria-label to each nav element: <nav aria-label="Primary navigation">, <nav aria-label="Footer links">

uxIssues.description: Page mixes <button> elements and <a> links styled as buttons for primary CTAs. This creates inconsistent interaction patterns.
uxIssues.recommendation: Use <button> for actions (submitting, triggering behavior) and <a href> for navigation. Avoid styling <a> tags as buttons for non-navigation actions.

uxIssues.description: Icon-only button detected with no text label or tooltip. Users must recall the meaning of the icon from memory.
uxIssues.recommendation: Add a visible label or tooltip: <button aria-label="Add to cart"><svg>...</svg> Add to Cart</button>

uxIssues.description: Primary navigation has 60 links, exceeding the cognitive load limit of 7±2 items.
uxIssues.recommendation: Group navigation items into logical categories. Use dropdown menus or mega-menus to organize secondary pages under fewer top-level items.

a11yIssues.description: Image element is missing an alt attribute. Screen readers cannot convey its meaning.
a11yIssues.recommendation: Add a descriptive alt attribute: . For decorative images use alt="".

a11yIssues.description: No skip navigation link detected. Keyboard users must tab through the entire navigation menu on every page.
a11yIssues.recommendation: Add a skip link as the first focusable element: Skip to main content

a11yIssues.description: Button element has no accessible name (no text, no aria-label, no title). Screen readers will announce it as an unlabelled button.
a11yIssues.recommendation: Add descriptive text inside the button or use aria-label: <button aria-label="Close dialog">

a11yIssues.description: Button element has no accessible name (no text, no aria-label, no title). Screen readers will announce it as an unlabelled button.
a11yIssues.recommendation: Add descriptive text inside the button or use aria-label: <button aria-label="Close dialog">

a11yIssues.description: Page has 3 <h1> elements. Multiple H1 tags confuse screen readers and break document outline.
a11yIssues.recommendation: Use exactly one <h1> per page. Reorganize secondary headings as <h2> or <h3>.

personas.name: First-time Visitor
personas.friction: Some inconsistency in the interface creates minor confusion, but the main flow is discoverable.
personas.recommendation: Add contextual hints for form fields and improve CTA hierarchy. Consider a welcome modal for first-time visitors.

personas.name: Elderly User
personas.friction: Clear typography and simple layout make this page accessible and comfortable for elderly users.
personas.recommendation: Maintain current accessibility practices. Consider adding a font-size toggle for users who need larger text.

personas.name: Power User
personas.friction: Core efficiency features are missing in some areas but the main workflow is functional.
personas.recommendation: Add persistent breadcrumb navigation and improve search functionality. Consider adding keyboard navigation hints.

personas.name: Visually Impaired User
personas.role: Screen reader and assistive technology user
personas.friction: Good accessibility practices make this page usable with screen readers. Minor improvements can be made.
personas.recommendation: Conduct regular screen reader testing. Consider adding 'aria-live' regions for dynamic content updates.

personas.name: Frequent Customer
personas.friction: Some inconsistencies slow down repeat tasks but core functionality works reliably.
personas.recommendation: Ensure navigation consistency across all pages. Add breadcrumbs to help returning users track their location.
